BandSeatingChart.heroTitle: Free Online Band Seating Chart Maker.
BandSeatingChart.heroDescription: Free online band seating chart maker for clean semicircle layouts. Set rows and seat counts, switch between top or bottom direction, and export a rehearsal-ready seating plan instantly.
BandSeatingChart.whyItWorksTitle: Build a readable band semicircle around the decisions that matter.
BandSeatingChart.whyItWorksDescription: Most band seating plans start with a half circle, not a freeform map. This maker stays focused on row structure, seat totals, top-or-bottom direction, and a clear conductor area inside the arc.
BandSeatingChart.comparisonPoint1: Enter row labels and seat counts in a simple table, then let the preview generate aligned half-circle rows automatically.
BandSeatingChart.comparisonPoint2: Switch the layout between top and bottom instead of managing arbitrary rotation values that are rarely needed for rehearsal seating.
BandSeatingChart.comparisonPoint3: Export a clean layout with solid arc guides, hollow square seats, and a centered conductor platform for rehearsal sheets or classroom handouts.
BandSeatingChart.previewDescription1: The live preview uses solid semicircle lines and hollow square seats so every row stays readable even when the layout becomes denser.
BandSeatingChart.previewDescription2: A rectangular conductor platform sits inside the semicircle and stays aligned with the base of the arc, which makes the overall direction easy to scan at a glance.
BandSeatingChart.productHighlightsTitle: Made for quick semicircle planning, not overbuilt seating maps.
BandSeatingChart.feature1Description: Use one table to define row labels and seat counts without adding musician names, instrument fields, or extra seat metadata.
BandSeatingChart.feature2Description: See the half-circle rows update immediately when you add a row, remove a row, or change seat totals.
BandSeatingChart.feature3Description: Switch the whole layout between top and bottom orientation without dealing with unnecessary angle controls.
BandSeatingChart.feature4Description: Keep a centered rectangular conductor platform inside the arc so the layout reads more like a practical rehearsal plan than a generic diagram.
BandSeatingChart.useCasesTitle: Useful for rehearsals, classrooms, and conductor-facing ensemble layouts.
BandSeatingChart.useCase1: Plan a concert band semicircle before rehearsal starts.
BandSeatingChart.useCase2: Sketch a top-facing or bottom-facing classroom ensemble layout for music educators.
BandSeatingChart.useCase3: Test different row counts before a recital, concert, or school performance.
BandSeatingChart.useCase4: Prepare a printable seating reference with a clear conductor position inside the arc.
BandSeatingChart.useCase5: Share a quick structure-first layout with students, section leaders, or event collaborators.
BandSeatingChart.useCase6: Block out a clean semicircle before adding names or instruments in another tool.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide1Point1: Your layout is mainly a semicircle or multi-row arc.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide1Point2: You only need row labels and seat counts to plan the structure.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide1Point3: You only need to choose whether the conductor side is on the top or the bottom.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide1Point4: You need a clean visual faster than drawing arcs, seats, and podiums manually in presentation software.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide2Point1: You need per-seat assignments like musician names, instruments, or notes.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide2Point2: Your venue uses complex zone pricing or ticket availability states.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide2Point3: You need left-right or arbitrary-angle placement for irregular room geometry.
BandSeatingChart.decisionGuide2Point4: You want a full auditorium seating map rather than a conductor-centered band arc layout.
BandSeatingChart.faqTitle: Everything people ask before building a band semicircle.
BandSeatingChart.faq1Answer: It controls the essentials: row labels, seat counts, row spacing, seat size, and whether the layout faces the top or the bottom.
BandSeatingChart.faq2Question: Can each row have a different number of seats?
BandSeatingChart.faq2Answer: Yes. Every row is edited independently in the table, so outer rows can hold more seats than inner rows.
BandSeatingChart.faq3Question: Can I switch the layout direction?
BandSeatingChart.faq3Answer: Yes. The current version keeps the choice intentionally simple: top or bottom. That covers the common conductor-facing setups without adding unnecessary rotation controls.
BandSeatingChart.faq4Question: Does it include musician names or instrument assignments?
BandSeatingChart.faq4Answer: Not in this version. The tool stays focused on the core semicircle structure so it remains fast and easy to edit.
BandSeatingChart.faq5Question: Is this for theater ticket seating maps?
BandSeatingChart.faq5Answer: Not really. It is better suited to band, rehearsal, or classroom seating layouts where rows, seat totals, and conductor position define the main structure.
BandSeatingChart.faq6Question: Can I export the current layout?
BandSeatingChart.faq6Answer: Yes. Use the export button in the preview panel to download the current seating chart as a PNG.
BandSeatingChart.ctaTitle: Build a conductor-ready band semicircle in minutes.
BandSeatingChart.ctaDescription: Enter rows on the left, choose top or bottom direction on the right, and use the live preview to turn a rough idea into a clean seating plan.
BandSeatingChart.editorEmptyState: Add at least one row with a seat count greater than zero to generate the seating chart.
BandSeatingChart.editorRotationHint: 0 means stage on the right. Use 90 for bottom, 180 for left, and 270 for top.
